stars.description: v1 of Asimov, an open-source humanoid robot
stars.highlights: Asimov-v1 is an open-source humanoid robot project, providing hardware and software designs for building a full-scale humanoid. It includes CAD files, control software, and assembly instructions, aiming to democratize humanoid robotics.
stars.worthReading: For AI leaders interested in embodied AI and robotics, this repo offers a rare open-source humanoid platform that can accelerate research in robot learning and control.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is high (new, low stars but rising), relevance is high (embodied AI/agent frontier), concreteness is high (real hardware/software artifact), giving 3+4+2=9, but clamped to 7 due to early stage uncertainty.

stars.highlights: OpenExo is an open-source exoskeleton project that provides hardware designs and control software for building lower-limb exoskeletons. It aims to democratize access to exoskeleton technology for rehabilitation and augmentation.
stars.worthReading: This project bridges robotics and AI, offering a rare open-source hardware platform that could integrate with AI-driven control systems, making it a unique resource for researchers and makers.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (273 stars, niche but not trending), relevance is low (exoskeletons are not frontier AI), and concreteness is high (working hardware/software), resulting in a score of 4.
